reasoning: The stock has undergone extreme downward momentum, falling over 20% in the last week (from >$12 to $9.67). This plummet centers around Paramount Skydance's (PSKY) massive $110 billion hostile—and heavily debt-financed ($54 billion)—takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). As a result, BofA downgraded their price target to $11, and Fitch downgraded PSKY's credit to "junk" status on March 12, adding severe pressure. While shares routinely stage dead-cat bounces when heavily oversold (and mathematical modeling projects a ~59% chance of reversion), institutional selling pressure due to the junk downgrade and debt concerns could easily drag through the coming week. The highly anticipated WBD shareholder meeting scheduled for Friday, March 20 could also create final volatile downward pressure just before the Friday close (which will serve as the March 22 weekend resolution price). Balancing the oversold technicals (which favor a bounce) with the horrendous fundamental and momentum picture (which favor continued bleed), I estimate a slightly bearish 45% probability that the price will close higher than $9.67.
